title: Is the 10% EBITDA Margin Target Finally Within Reach for Chewy?
text: Chewy's adjusted EBITDA margin for fiscal year 2024 reached 4.8%, reflecting year-over-year expansion of approximately 150 basis points. For full-year 2025, management narrowed adjusted EBITDA margin guidance to 5.6%-5.7%, reflecting a year-over-year expansion of about 90 basis points. Q1 2025 adjusted EBITDA margin of 6.2% represented a 50 basis point improvement over the 5.7% reported in Q1 2024. Management is increasingly confident in its ability to reach its long-term adjusted EBITDA margin target of 10%. With accelerating margin expansion, improving cost discipline, and multiple structural profit drivers in place, Chewy appears well-positioned to steadily close the remaining gap. About 60% of margin uplift is expected from gross margin improvements, driven by high-margin contributors like sponsored ads, shift toward premium categories, and the health ecosystem, with the remaining 40% coming from SG&A leverage. The path to 10% EBITDA margin demonstrates Chewy's progression from a growth-focused company to a profitability-focused business.
